extraction.one_line_summary: KRISTOFF Alexander won the final sprint on the Champs-Élysées after LAMPERT Yves was caught inside 200m from an aggressive late solo attack.
tactical_patterns: LAMPERT Yves solo break caught only 200m from line reveals sprinter teams' willingness to gamble late on Paris stage.
KRISTOFF Alexander broke four-year stage drought on bumpy Champs finish, suggesting technical surface skills separate equal sprinters in Paris.
THOMAS Geraint rode defensively after second time check in stage 20 ITT despite leading, banking time rather than stage win.

rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: positive: Won Paris stage after five prior top-fives this Tour, proving superior traction skills on cobbled uphill drag finish.
rider_signals.DEGENKOLB John: positive: Shoulder-to-shoulder sprint battle for second shows retained explosive speed despite challenging mountain survival.
rider_signals.LAMPAERT Yves: positive: Aggressive late solo move nearly stole sprinters' showcase, indicating willingness to gamble over team sprint obligations.
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: Won Tour after freewheeling descents in stage 20 ITT once establishing insurmountable lead at second time check.
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: Nearly won stage 20 ITT to secure third overall after being written off podium contention earlier in race.
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: neutral: Second overall without stage win highlights consistency over explosive race-defining attacks.
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: Crash few days prior likely cost him Paris sprint victory according to commentary.
rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: neutral: Right-hand positioning in sprint showed late speed but insufficient to challenge KRISTOFF's superior line.
